content.full_text:
July 20, 1976 
Mr. Ernest Hawkins 
Assistant Registrar of Voters 
3700 Branch Center Road, Suite C 
Sacramento, California 9S827 
Dear Mr. Hawkins: 
This letter is to confirm my advice to Myrtle Aguilar of your 
staff concerning Government Code Section BlOOSCb). 
According to our legal division's interpretation of Section 
8l00SCb), committees which support or oppose more than one candi-
date for 8uperior eourt ~udge, member of the State Legislature or 
Board of Equalization, must file campaign disclosure statements 
with the following officials: 
Original and one copy - Secretary of State; 
Two copies 
Two copies 
- Clerk of the one county with the 
largest population (in whole or 
in part) in all of the election 
districts in which the candidates 
the filer supports or opposes 
seek nomination or electioni 
- Either the clerk of the county of 
the filing committee's domicile 
or the clerks of every county in 
which the candidates supported or 
opposed reside. 
A committee which files its first pre-election statement with 
Sacramento County is not also required to file copies of its second 
pre-election and post-election statements with Sacramento County if 
it is not the largest county by population in the election 
of candidates supported or opposed in the periods covered by such 
reports. 
a 
a campaign statement 
a candidate 
a 
tion 
county clerks 
supported or 
larger county 
of 
filed with them: (1) con-


-
2 -
trolled committees of candidates for office which are voted upon by 
the voters of their county; (2) committees domiciled in their county 
filing in connection with state elections; and (3) controlled commit-
tees of state assembly and senate candidates when their county is 
the largest by population in whole or in part in the election districts 
of such candidates. It is our staff's present view that county clerks 
are not required to contact non-filers other than those in one of the 
three categories described above. 
Further, the staff believes that committees which failed to file 
in Sacramento County for reporting periods during which they supported 
or opposed candidates from election districts which include counties 
with population's larger than Sacramento's should not have been fined. 
If you would like to discuss this matter, please contact our 
staff. 
Sincerely, 
Nan Hambleton 
Special Compliance Representative 
NH:bw

sections.conclusion: Committees supporting or opposing more than one candidate for Superior Court Judge, State Legislature member, or Board of Equalization must file campaign disclosure statements with the Secretary of State (original and one copy), the clerk of the largest population county in the election districts involved (two copies), and either the domicile county clerk or all counties where supported/opposed candidates reside (two copies). Committees need not refile with Sacramento County if it is not the largest county by population in relevant election districts. County clerks are not required to contact non-filers outside three specified categories.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the filing requirements for political committees that support or oppose multiple candidates for Superior Court Judge, State Legislature, or Board of Equalization positions under Government Code Section 8100(b)?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC clarifies filing requirements for multi-candidate committees, specifying destinations for disclosure statements based on Secretary of State, largest population county, and domicile/candidate residence counties. Committees are not required to file with Sacramento County for all reporting periods if it is not the largest population county in the relevant election districts.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a formal advice letter without explicit QUESTION/CONCLUSION headers, but contains clear legal interpretation and guidance. Synthetic question and conclusion derived from body text. Minor OCR artifacts present (e.g., 'BlOOSCb)' likely meant to be '8100(b)') but context is clear.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the filing requirements for political committees that support or oppose multiple candidates for Superior Court Judge, State Legislature, or Board of Equalization positions under Government Code Section 8100(b)?

CONCLUSION: Committees supporting or opposing more than one candidate for Superior Court Judge, State Legislature member, or Board of Equalization must file campaign disclosure statements with the Secretary of State (original and one copy), the clerk of the largest population county in the election districts involved (two copies), and either the domicile county clerk or all counties where supported/opposed candidates reside (two copies). Committees need not refile with Sacramento County if it is not the largest county by population in relevant election districts. County clerks are not required to contact non-filers outside three specified categories.

A committee which files its first pre-election statement with Sacramento County is not also required to file copies of its second pre-election and post-election statements with Sacramento County if it is not the largest county by population in the election of candidates supported or opposed in the periods covered by such reports. a a campaign statement a candidate a tion county clerks supported or larger county of filed with them: (1) con- - 2 - trolled committees of candidates for office which are voted upon by the voters of their county; (2) committees domiciled in their county filing in connection with state elections; and (3) controlled commit- tees of state assembly and senate candidates when their county is the largest by population in whole or in part in the election districts of such candidates. It is our staff's present view that county clerks are not required to contact non-filers other than those in one of the three categories described above. Further, the staff believes that committees which failed to file in Sacramento County for reporting periods during which they supported or opposed candidates from election districts which include counties with population's larger than Sacramento's should not have been fined. If you would like to discuss this matter, please contact our staff. Sincerely, Nan Hambleton Special Compliance Representative NH:bw
embedding.summary: FPPC Special Compliance Representative confirms filing requirements under Government Code Section 8100(b) for committees supporting or opposing multiple candidates for statewide offices, addressing which officials require campaign disclosure statements and clarifying county clerk responsibilities.
